ddls:
CREATE TABLE "map_layers" (
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each map layer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the map layer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the map layer */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of map layer */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Status of the map layer (enabled or disabled) */,
  "default_layer" INTEGER /* Indicates if the layer is the default layer */,
  "tile_url" TEXT /* URL of the tile server for this layer */,
  "min_zoom_level" INTEGER /* Minimum zoom level for this layer */,
  "max_zoom_level" INTEGER /* Maximum zoom level for this layer */,
  "attribution_text" TEXT /* Attribution text for this layer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("layer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_map_layers_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES layer_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "layer_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each layer type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the layer type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the layer type */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Group ID to categorize layer types (e.g., base layers, overlay layers) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role ID assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "layer_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the map layer being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the layer */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the layer was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location where the layer was accessed (e.g., IP address, city) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_layer_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_layer_access_layer_id FOREIGN KEY ("layer_id") REFERENCES map_layers ("layer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "layer_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the map layer changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change made (e.g., add, edit, delete) */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_comment" TEXT /* Comment describing the change made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_layer_history_layer_id FOREIGN KEY ("layer_id") REFERENCES map_layers ("layer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_layer_history_changed_by FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "layer_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the map layer */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date when the layer was created */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the layer was last updated */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the layer data */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the layer data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_layer_metadata_layer_id FOREIGN KEY ("layer_id") REFERENCES map_layers ("layer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_level" INTEGER /* Hierarchy level of the role (e.g., 1 for admin, 2 for user) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "scope" TEXT /* Scope of the permission (e.g., specific layer, all layers) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "teams" (
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each team */,
  "team_name" TEXT /* Name of the team */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the team */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("team_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_teams" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the team (e.g., member, leader) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "team_id")
)
